text.title: JUnit Testing Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Common agent mistakes when writing Spring Boot JUnit tests: wrong mock layer, argument matcher mixing, context cache thrash.

antipattern.description: Mocking the wrong layer: agent mocks UserRepository in a @WebMvcTest controller test where UserService is also mocked — the entire flow is stubbed, testing nothing.
antipattern.reason: @WebMvcTest is for testing the controller + HTTP binding. Only the direct dependency of the controller (the service) should be mocked. Mocking the repository in this context means no real behavior is exercised.

antipattern.description: Argument matcher mixing: verify(svc).save(user, any()) next to a non-matcher literal argument throws InvalidUseOfMatchersException.
antipattern.reason: Mockito requires all arguments in a verify/when call to be matchers OR all to be literals — mixing is illegal. Always use eq(value) to wrap a literal when any other argument uses a matcher.

antipattern.description: @MockBean on a non-Spring-managed class: agent annotates a class that is not registered as a bean; Spring fails to find it and the test crashes with a cryptic context error.
antipattern.reason: @MockBean creates a Mockito mock and registers it as a Spring bean. If the class is not a bean (e.g. a utility class), use @Mock instead (plain Mockito, no Spring).

antipattern.description: @SpringBootTest scattered across every test class to avoid writing @WebMvcTest or @DataJpaTest slices.
antipattern.reason: Each unique @MockBean set forces Spring to create a new application context. Reusing one full-stack @SpringBootTest and many slice tests is dramatically faster.

antipattern.description: static mocking via mockStatic without try-with-resources: the static mock remains active across subsequent tests, causing flaky suite behavior.
antipattern.reason: MockedStatic must be closed after each test; try-with-resources guarantees cleanup. Forgetting it leaves LocalDateTime.now() or similar stubbed globally.

antipattern.description: DB-dependent test missing @Transactional or @Sql cleanup: seed inserted inside one test is visible to the next, causing duplicate key failures on repeated runs.
antipattern.reason: @DataJpaTest rolls back per test by default. @SpringBootTest does not. Add @Transactional or @Sql(executionPhase=AFTER_TEST_METHOD) to clean up explicitly.

antipattern.description: Coverage without assertions: agent calls service.foo() with verifyNoMoreInteractions() but no assertThat on the return value — test passes regardless of behavior.
antipattern.reason: Line coverage tracks which lines ran, not whether the output was correct. Mutation testing (Pitest) catches assertion-free tests by verifying tests fail when behavior changes.
